reasons: Angela's shield support helps Hanabi maintain safer damage uptime during enemy engage windows.
Hanabi benefits strongly from external shielding because her shield state supports a control-resistance style of fighting.

proof.summary: Angela can shield Hanabi during a danger window, helping Hanabi keep attacking while resisting control pressure through shield uptime.
worksBestWhen: Angela shields Hanabi during real enemy engage.
Hanabi has enough positioning space to keep attacking.
failureCases: Angela attaches too early and the shield expires before danger.
Enemy burst breaks through before Hanabi can use the window.

reasons: Lolita can block ranged attacks and projectiles that threaten Hanabi's backline position.
Lolita's protective formation gives Hanabi more time to attack in front-to-back fights.

proof.summary: Lolita protects Hanabi's firing lane, letting Hanabi continue dealing damage from behind the frontline.
worksBestWhen: Hanabi positions behind Lolita instead of beside her.
failureCases: Lolita's shield is baited before Hanabi's real damage window.

reasons: Diggie can protect Hanabi from heavy crowd-control engage during important teamfight windows.
Hanabi benefits from anti-engage support because her teamfight value depends on continuous basic-attack uptime.

proof.summary: Diggie helps Hanabi survive enemy control attempts so she can keep attacking from the backline.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves protection for the main engage.
Hanabi stays close enough to receive protection.
failureCases: Diggie's key skill is baited early.
Hanabi is caught outside the protection area.

reasons: Estes can keep Hanabi healthy during front-to-back teamfights, helping her maintain basic-attack uptime.
Hanabi benefits from sustained teamfight formations because she wants time to attack safely and punish grouped enemies.

proof.summary: Hanabi plays as a backline teamfight marksman, while Estes adds healing that helps her stay active through longer fights.
worksBestWhen: Hanabi stays close enough to receive Estes's healing.
The team plays a grouped front-to-back formation.
Hanabi already has safe spacing and does not need to chase forward.
failureCases: Hanabi is instantly bursted before healing matters.
Hanabi positions too far from Estes or gets flanked.

reasons: Tigreal can group enemies, increasing Hanabi's chance to get strong teamfight bounce value.
Tigreal's frontline control gives Hanabi safer time to attack after the engage.

proof.summary: Tigreal groups and controls enemies, while Hanabi attacks from behind to punish clustered targets.
worksBestWhen: Hanabi is already in safe attack range.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Hanabi.
Enemies spread and deny bounce value.
